title: endiandra sphaerica
intro: Endiandra sphaerica is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, commonly known as the 'Yellow Bells' or 'Yellow Flowering Ash', native to the eastern coast of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Endiandra sphaerica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Endiandra sphaerica are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
